task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/ranger_station_browser_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Open the web browser and access its settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a web browser (e.g., Firefox, Chrome) open with the settings, preferences, or configuration page visible.
Screenshot shows evidence of browser cleanup, such as a dialog for clearing browsing data, history, cache, or removing unnecessary extensions/bookmarks.

task_completion.description: Optimize browser for a low-bandwidth satellite connection
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows browser settings adjusted for low bandwidth, such as disabling image loading, blocking media autoplay, or enabling a data-saving mode.
Screenshot shows browser settings related to offline resilience being modified, such as increasing the web cache size, enabling offline storage, or configuring service workers.

task_completion.description: Set up rapid access to critical tools
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the browser interface with newly added bookmarks in the bookmarks bar, pinned tabs, or a customized homepage containing links to critical tools.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual web browser or its legitimate configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine browser windows, settings menus, or valid configuration file edits (e.g., prefs.js) rather than a text file simply claiming the task is done.

integrity.description: The agent did not just echo a success message to the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent actually applied the settings in the browser environment and didn't just print 'Browser optimized' in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent applied settings based on the spec file rather than generic guesses.
integrity.visual_evidence: The configured settings and bookmarks should logically correspond to a backcountry permit desk (e.g., permit portals, weather tools) rather than generic placeholders.
